words.clue: Midline depression on the ventral pons surface for the basilar artery
Rounded elevation on the 4th ventricle floor caused by CN VII fibres looping over CN VI nucleus
Syndrome with full consciousness but complete quadriplegia, only vertical eye movements preserved
Largest cerebellar peduncle connecting pons to cerebellum
Cranial nerve that exits at the cerebellopontine angle along with CN VIII
Pigmented noradrenergic nucleus visible on the 4th ventricular floor
Fibre bundle near midline essential for conjugate horizontal eye movements
Tumour at the cerebellopontine angle, most common type
